turns.user_message: Someone just sighed when I shared my thoughts and told me I always overcomplicate simple things, which is honestly something I hear way too often. I tried to laugh it off, but the words stuck with me all afternoon. I keep replaying the moment in my head, wondering if they are completely right about me. It feels like I naturally turn straightforward situations into messy puzzles. I just wish I could trust my own instincts without second-guessing every single choice.

That same dismissive tone keeps echoing in my mind every time I try to speak up lately. I notice myself shrinking back before I even finish a sentence, convinced I am just wasting everyone's time. It has become a heavy routine where I mentally edit my thoughts until they sound completely hollow. I feel like a broken record that only plays the wrong notes. Every interaction leaves me exhausted because I am constantly trying to prove I am not as useless as I sound.

That one casual remark has completely shattered whatever confidence I had left, and I cannot shake the heavy weight of it. I catch myself apologizing for simply existing in the room, terrified that my presence is just another inconvenience for people around me. It feels like I am fundamentally flawed in a way that no amount of effort will ever fix. I watch everyone else navigate life so effortlessly while I trip over my own shadow. I am just so tired of feeling like a constant disappointment to myself and everyone else.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
